systemPrompt: Identify large files and compute total size in a temporary workspace

tools.shell: Generic shell skill that can propose and reason about Linux commands to inspect file sizes and filter them in a temporary workspace.

prompts: In a temporary workspace you have these files and sizes: build/output.bin (1200 KB), build/cache.tmp (300 KB), logs/app.log (700 KB), README.md (50 KB). Using Linux commands conceptually (e.g. find, -size, etc.), what command would you run from the workspace root to list only the paths of files larger than 600 KB? Answer only with the command.
Assuming your command correctly lists build/output.bin and logs/app.log (and no others), what is the total size in KB of the files that would be listed? Answer only with the number.
Now, still conceptually, what command would you run to delete only those large files in one go, from the workspace root? Answer only with the command.
